description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer codifying an account-lockout policy.  
A tab-separated audit file already exists:

/home/user/compliance/failed_logins.tsv

Its exact contents are:

username	ip_address	timestamp	attempts
alice	10.0.0.5	2023-08-01T12:01:22Z	3
bob	10.0.0.6	2023-08-01T12:02:17Z	7
carol	10.0.0.7	2023-08-01T12:04:03Z	5
dave	10.0.0.8	2023-08-01T12:05:55Z	10

(Columns are separated by a single horizontal TAB. The first line is the header.)

Policy tooling only needs the username (column 1) and number of failed attempts (column 4) with **no header** to feed into an automated lockout routine.

TASK  
1. From the existing file, create a new file at  
   /home/user/compliance/analysis/lockout_candidates.tsv  

2. The new file must contain exactly the following four data lines, in the same order as the source, and must be tab-separated with **no header line**:

alice	3
bob	7
carol	5
dave	10

3. Accomplish the extraction using a single terminal command that relies on standard “cut/paste style” column manipulation utilities available on a typical Linux distribution (e.g. `cut`, `awk`, `paste`, etc.).  
   • Do not manually re-type the data.  
   • Overwrite the file if it already exists.  
   • Ensure the directory /home/user/compliance/analysis/ exists (create it if necessary).

The automated test will verify:

• That /home/user/compliance/analysis/lockout_candidates.tsv exists.  
• That it contains exactly four lines (no header).  
• That each line is the username followed by a single TAB and the attempts count, and in the order shown above.  
• That there is a trailing newline after the last line.

No other output or files are expected.
truth:
Pre-populated artefacts before the agent starts:

1. File: /home/user/compliance/failed_logins.tsv  
   Contents (with literal TAB characters between columns):

username	ip_address	timestamp	attempts
alice	10.0.0.5	2023-08-01T12:01:22Z	3
bob	10.0.0.6	2023-08-01T12:02:17Z	7
carol	10.0.0.7	2023-08-01T12:04:03Z	5
dave	10.0.0.8	2023-08-01T12:05:55Z	10

2. Directory /home/user/compliance/analysis/ is absent at start (so agent must create it or use mkdir -p).

Expected artefacts after successful completion:

File: /home/user/compliance/analysis/lockout_candidates.tsv  
Exact contents (each “⇥” represents a literal TAB, and file ends with “\n”):

alice⇥3
bob⇥7
carol⇥5
dave⇥10

(That is, four lines, each ending with LF, no header, no extra spaces.)

Permissions: default user-writable regular file (mode 0644 acceptable).

No other new files or directories should be created.
